_metadata.description: make_AGENTS skill — generates tool-agnostic AGENTS.md project context files. Sibling to make_agent.md and make_gem.md.
_metadata.instructions: MD = the why (mission, principles, narrative). JSON = the what (required/optional sections, template strings, compact boilerplate, validation). Update both files together.

skill_details.description: Generates AGENTS.md project context files that are tool-agnostic and embed the behavioral discipline.

agent_type.description: Workflow-based skill: identify project context → generate AGENTS.md → propose write → wait for confirmation → write file.

behavioral_discipline._description: make_AGENTS itself operates under the behavioral discipline. Source: knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md/.json.

_skipped_principles_rationale.P-005: Single-step workflow (one AGENTS.md per invocation), no decomposition needed.

inputs.name: project_root
inputs.format: Absolute or relative path to the project's root directory

inputs.name: existing_claude_md
inputs.format: Path to an existing CLAUDE.md to migrate from (optional)

outputs.name: agents_md
outputs.format: Generated AGENTS.md written at project root
outputs.conditions: Created if absent, or proposed-as-update if present (per P-002 — never silently overwrites)

outputs.name: discipline_files_packaged
outputs.format: knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md + knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json copied into the TARGET project's knowledge/ folder (with snapshot header indicating origin commit-sha)
outputs.conditions: Required when the target repo does not already have these files (either at canonical knowledge/ path OR at a cloned-subdirectory path like make_ai_agents/knowledge/). Skipped if already present; the Working Style pointer is then updated to whichever local path resolves. Without this packaging step, the Working Style pointer is a broken reference.

io_contract.side_effects: Creates or updates AGENTS.md at project root after user confirmation. Copies knowledge/behavioral_discipline.{md,json} into the target's knowledge/ folder unless already present (with snapshot header for traceability). Does NOT delete the existing CLAUDE.md — that is a separate follow-up step.

agents_md_required_sections._description: Sections that EVERY generated AGENTS.md must contain, in this order. Used by make_AGENTS to generate and by validation to check.

sections.heading: # <Project Name>
sections.description: H1 with project name + one-line description below. Tag line, not paragraph.

sections.heading: ## Project Purpose
sections.description: What the repo IS (3-5 bullets) and what it is NOT (2-4 bullets). Includes audience.

sections.heading: ## Structure
sections.description: Annotated folder layout in a code block. Reference, not exhaustive — top-level + key subfolders only.

sections.heading: ## Working Style
sections.description: Behavioral discipline pointer (substituted from compact_boilerplate.working_style_template) plus any project-specific rules.
must_include: discipline pointer to knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md or equivalent

sections.heading: ## Active Context
sections.description: Current state, in-flight work, recent major changes, open issues. Date-stamped.
must_include: _Last updated: YYYY-MM-DD_ line at the top of the section

agents_md_optional_sections._description: Sections to include only when the inclusion criteria apply.

sections.heading: ## Domain Terms
sections.include_when: The project has non-obvious vocabulary that an LLM would interpret wrong without explicit definitions.

sections.heading: ## External System Lessons
sections.include_when: The project interacts with external systems (Canvas API, GitHub API, Stripe, etc.) with non-obvious behaviors discovered through use.
sections.format: Per-system entry: Behavior / Why it matters / How to handle

sections.heading: ## Migration Notes
sections.include_when: The project is mid-migration (CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md, library swap, framework upgrade). Remove section once migration completes.

sections.heading: ## Existing Tooling
sections.include_when: The project has scripts or utilities the LLM should reuse before generating new code.
sections.format: Tool / Purpose / When to use table

agents_md_template._description: The skeleton AGENTS.md the make_AGENTS skill substitutes into. Placeholders use {{double_braces}}.

compact_boilerplate._description: Templates substituted into the generated AGENTS.md. The discipline pointer is the most important — it propagates the behavioral discipline from this repo into every project that uses an AGENTS.md generated by this skill.
compact_boilerplate.working_style_template:
This project follows the behavioral discipline defined in `knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md` (if present in this repo) or the equivalent discipline loaded via the host tool's skill system. **Read that file before doing non-trivial work in this repo.**

The four no-override principles — **P-001 Read Before Claiming, P-003 Stop on Defect, P-007 Pull Don't Push, P-010 Respect Intent** — apply unconditionally; the other six (P-002, P-004, P-005, P-006, P-008, P-009) have documented override conditions in the discipline file.

pre_run_checklist: project_root exists and is a directory
knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md is readable (or equivalent discipline source declared)
If existing_claude_md is provided, it is readable
post_run_checklist: All required sections present in order: title, project_purpose, structure, working_style, active_context
Working Style section contains a pointer to the behavioral discipline
Active Context section starts with a `_Last updated: YYYY-MM-DD_` line
No tool-specific language in the file body (no 'Claude Code does X' style instructions)
success_criteria: Generated AGENTS.md is tool-agnostic (readable by any agentic dev tool)
Behavioral discipline embed present in Working Style
Each optional section present has its inclusion criteria met

test_cases.name: Generate AGENTS.md for a fresh project
test_cases.expected_output: AGENTS.md created with all 5 required sections, no optional sections, discipline pointer in Working Style.

test_cases.name: Generate AGENTS.md for a project with existing CLAUDE.md
test_cases.expected_output: AGENTS.md created. Workspace rules from CLAUDE.md migrated to project_purpose + working_style. Migration Notes section included documenting CLAUDE.md deletion as a follow-up step.

test_cases.name: Reject silently overwriting an existing AGENTS.md
test_cases.expected_output: Per P-002: skill proposes the new AGENTS.md as a diff against the existing file, waits for confirmation. Does not silently overwrite.

qc_checks._description: Skill-specific validation checks. These are NOT the BD-QC checks (those live in knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json) — these are checks specific to whether the GENERATED AGENTS.md meets the contract.

checks.name: All required sections present
checks.rule: Generated AGENTS.md contains all 5 required sections from agents_md_required_sections in the specified order.

checks.name: Working Style contains discipline pointer
checks.rule: Working Style section references `knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md` or an equivalent discipline source. Without this, the discipline doesn't propagate from this repo to the project.

checks.name: Active Context has date stamp
checks.rule: Active Context section starts with `_Last updated: YYYY-MM-DD_`. The date stamp signals freshness; without it, stale context goes undetected.

checks.name: No tool-specific language
checks.rule: Generated AGENTS.md body does not contain tool-specific instructions (e.g., 'When using Claude Code...', 'In Cursor only...'). Tool-specific guidance belongs in tool-specific config files, not AGENTS.md.

checks.name: Optional sections meet inclusion criteria
checks.rule: If an optional section is present, the project must satisfy that section's `include_when` criterion in agents_md_optional_sections. Empty optional sections (e.g., a Domain Terms table with placeholder rows) fail this check.

checks.name: Discipline files co-located with AGENTS.md
checks.rule: The Working Style section's discipline pointer must resolve to a file that actually exists in the target repo. PASS conditions (any one): (a) `<target_repo>/knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md` and `behavioral_discipline.json` both exist at the canonical path; (b) the AGENTS.md pointer references a cloned-subdirectory path (e.g., `make_ai_agents/knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md`) AND that path resolves to a file; (c) the host tool's skill system loads the equivalent discipline at runtime (declare this explicitly in Working Style if relying on this path). FAIL: the pointer references a path that doesn't resolve. Without local discipline files, the pointer is a broken reference and the discipline does not propagate to agents working in this repo. Severity critical because the entire purpose of make_AGENTS is to propagate the discipline — a broken pointer defeats the skill.

changes: Initial bare-bones make_AGENTS skill — sibling to make_agent and make_gem.
Defines required and optional sections for AGENTS.md.
Embeds behavioral discipline pointer (compact_boilerplate.working_style_template) so every generated AGENTS.md inherits the discipline.
5 skill-specific QC checks (AGENTS-QC-001 through AGENTS-QC-005). AGENTS-QC-006 added 2026-05-13 for discipline-file co-location enforcement.
Skill itself operates as interaction_pattern: single_write_workflow with applicable_principles P-001, P-002, P-003, P-004, P-006, P-007, P-008, P-009, P-010 (skip P-005).
